questions.prompt: How should Chromium/Chrome be shipped for all `runner_mode: container` images?

options.label: Bake into core manifest (apt-packages-core.txt) — every rebuild gets browser + driver; larger image for everyone
Keep image lean — document + template example using container_runner_image.extra_apt_packages only (no default browser)
Bake into core + document Rails/Capybara (recommended if this is a first-class CI target)

content.text:
Baking the browser into the core manifest. Creating a concise implementation plan that includes adding `--shm-size` so Chromium/Selenium runs reliably in Docker.

[REDACTED]

input.name: Chrome in container runner
input.overview: Add Chromium and Chromedriver to the embedded core apt manifest for the gh-sr container runner image, increase Docker shared memory for stable browser jobs, and document Rails/Capybara/Selenium usage paths and flags.
input.plan:
# Chrome/Chromium in agentic container runner

## Context

The container runner image is built from [`internal/runner/agentic-runner-image/Dockerfile`](internal/runner/agentic-runner-image/Dockerfile): Ubuntu 24.04 → core packages from [`internal/runner/agentic-runner-image/apt-packages-core.txt`](internal/runner/agentic-runner-image/apt-packages-core.txt) (embedded in [`internal/runner/assets.go`](internal/runner/assets.go)) → optional `container_runner_image.extra_apt_packages` via [`apt-packages-extra.txt`](internal/runner/agentic-runner-image/Dockerfile) generated at build time.

Jobs run **inside** the outer runner container (not only in inner DinD). Installing Chromium there satisfies Rails system tests driven by Selenium/Capybara against a real browser.

You chose **baking packages into the core manifest** (not opt-in only).

## Implementation

### 1. Core apt manifest

Add these Ubuntu 24.04 package names (one per line, alphabetically with existing entries):

- **`chromium`** — browser binary (typical path `/usr/bin/chromium`; package may also provide a `chromium-browser` compatibility symlink depending on Ubuntu packaging).
- **`chromium-chromedriver`** — OS-packaged driver matched to that Chromium build (avoids Selenium/driver skew).

No Dockerfile change is required beyond the manifest: the existing `RUN` that consumes `apt-packages-core.txt` already installs listed packages.

**Trade-off:** Image size grows noticeably (Chromium stack + deps); every container-mode runner pays this cost.

### 2. Docker `--shm-size` for the runner container

Chrome/Chromium often crashes or flakes under Docker’s default **64 MiB `/dev/shm`**. The runner outer container is created in [`internal/runner/container.go`](internal/runner/container.go) (`docker create` ~lines 198–221). Today there is **no** `--shm-size`.

Add something like **`--shm-size=2g`** to that `docker create` invocation so headless/full browser stacks used during Actions jobs have adequate shared memory.

Update the mirrored expectations in [`internal/runner/container_test.go`](internal/runner/container_test.go) (`TestDockerRunArgShape` builds the same command shape manually).

**Trade-off:** Each running runner container reserves more host RAM backing shm; 2 GB is a common CI default (tune if hosts are memory-constrained).

### 3. Documentation (Rails / Selenium)

Add a short subsection (appropriate place: [`docs/content/guides/agentic-workflows.md`](docs/content/guides/agentic-workflows.md) near container-mode content, or [`docs/content/configuration.md`](docs/content/configuration.md) if you prefer a “browser testing” callout) covering:

- **Binary paths:** e.g. set `CHROME_BIN` / Capybara Selenium `browser_options` to the installed Chromium binary (`chromium` package on Noble).
- **Container flags:** In CI, Chromium/Chrome often needs `--no-sandbox` (and sometimes `--disable-gpu`); with larger `--shm-size`, `--disable-dev-shm-usage` is less critical but still a common escape hatch.
- **Rebuild:** After changing the core manifest, operators should run `gh sr rebuild` for container-mode runners so the image is rebuilt (existing behavior; see current docs around image tag / revision).

### 4. Verification

- `go test ./...` (and `go vet ./...` per project habit).
- No new integration test against a real browser is strictly required; optional: a tiny test that embedded `apt-packages-core.txt` contains the new lines (if you want regression protection—lightweight string check in an existing embed test pattern).

## Out of scope (unless you want follow-up)

- **Google Chrome** (non-Chromium) from Google’s apt repo: would require an extra `RUN` in the Dockerfile (not expressible as a single line in `apt-packages-core.txt` alone). Chromium is the standard approach for Linux CI.
- **Inner AWF/sandbox containers:** If Rails tests run inside *another* Docker layer spawned by gh-aw, that inner image would still need its own browser stack; this plan only covers the **gh-sr outer runner image** where your Actions job shell runs.

## Mermaid: where the browser runs

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  host[Host_Docker]
  outer[gh_sr_runner_container]
  job[Actions_job_shell]
  host --> outer
  outer --> job
  job --> chromium["Chromium_+_chromedriver_in_core_image"]
```

todos.content: Add --shm-size to docker create in container.go; update container_test.go
Document Rails/Selenium/Capybara paths, flags, and rebuild note in guides or configuration.md
Run go test ./... and go vet ./...

input.new_string:
In `gh sr status` (and the dashboard), the **IMAGE** column shows the Docker image reference; its tag is the **GitHub Actions runner** package version (not the gh-sr CLI version). The **BUILD** column compares a short **layout revision** baked into the image at `docker build` time with the revision your current `gh`/`gh sr` binary expects: **ok (…)** means the running container’s image matches this gh-sr build; **stale (…)** means you should run `gh sr rebuild` (or rebuild after upgrading gh-sr); **?** means the image predates revision labels (rebuild once to stamp them).

**Chromium / Selenium / Rails system tests:** The core image includes the Ubuntu `chromium` and `chromium-chromedriver` packages. Use `/usr/bin/chromium` (or the `chromium` / `chromium-browser` symlinks the package provides) as the browser binary—e.g. set `CHROME_BIN` for tools that look for Google Chrome, or Capybara Selenium `browser_options` to point at that path. The matching driver is on `PATH` as `chromedriver` from `chromium-chromedriver`. In CI containers, pass Chrome/Chromium args such as `--no-sandbox` and often `--disable-gpu`; with the runner’s `--shm-size=2g`, `--disable-dev-shm-usage` is usually optional but still a valid fallback. After a gh-sr upgrade that changes the core apt manifest, run `gh sr rebuild` so container-mode runners pick up the new image.

**When to use which mode:**

input.new_string: gh-sr will build the runner container image locally on first `gh sr setup` (Ubuntu 24.04 + Docker CE + gh-aw + dnsmasq + actions runner + Chromium and `chromium-chromedriver` for browser/system tests). Core apt dependencies live in the repo manifest `internal/runner/agentic-runner-image/apt-packages-core.txt` (baked into the gh-sr binary). Optional tools: add a global `container_runner_image.extra_apt_packages` list in `runners.yml` (Debian package names only). Subsequent setups skip the build if the image for that tag already exists; after changing the extra list or upgrading gh-sr when the core manifest changed, run `gh sr rebuild` for container-mode runners if needed.

content.text:
### Summary

1. **Core image packages** — Added `chromium` and `chromium-chromedriver` to [`internal/runner/agentic-runner-image/apt-packages-core.txt`](internal/runner/agentic-runner-image/apt-packages-core.txt) (after `ca-certificates`, before `curl`).

2. **Runner container** — In [`internal/runner/container.go`](internal/runner/container.go), `docker create` now includes `--shm-size=2g` (with a short comment on why). [`internal/runner/container_test.go`](internal/runner/container_test.go) mirrors that and asserts `--shm-size=2g`.

3. **Docs** — In [`docs/content/guides/agentic-workflows.md`](docs/content/guides/agentic-workflows.md): container-mode intro mentions Chromium + chromedriver; new **Chromium / Selenium / Rails system tests** paragraph covers paths, flags, and `gh sr rebuild`.

---

**[VERDICT]** Extended [`apt-packages-core.txt`](internal/runner/agentic-runner-image/apt-packages-core.txt); adjusted `setupContainer` `docker create` and [`TestDockerRunArgShape`](internal/runner/container_test.go) in [`container.go`](internal/runner/container.go); updated [`agentic-workflows.md`](docs/content/guides/agentic-workflows.md). **Build:** `go build ./...` OK. **Tests:** `go test ./...` OK; `go vet ./...` OK. **Watch:** Operators must run **`gh sr rebuild`** for existing container runners so hosts rebuild the image with Chromium; `--shm-size=2g` increases per-container shared-memory backing on the host (~2 GiB cap per runner container).

[REDACTED]
